basic_information.occupation: Part time work, training as a ___ for ___

medical_history.chief_complaint: "The past week I have been very depressed and I don't feel safe at home"
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Patient has a history of cocaine induced mood disorder with psychotic features. He was brought in by his godmother with complaints of worsening depression, auditory hallucinations (AH), visual hallucinations (VH), paranoia for 1 week. He relapsed on cocaine 3 days ago and reports stress from supporting his girlfriend financially. Patient stopped taking psychiatric medications, leading to increased symptoms. He was previously stable after a 3-day hospitalization until about a week ago when he began feeling low energy, insomnia, decreased appetite, poor concentration, and lack of motivation. In the past 3 days, he has developed racing thoughts, increasing AH and paranoia.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Historical diagnoses include cocaine induced mood disorder with psychotic features, anxiety, bipolar disorder, and schizophrenia. Reports 10 total inpatient admissions with 3 for depression. Current medications include seroquel, remeron, and zoloft; previously on depakote, cymbalta, and abilify. Past suicide attempt involved cutting his wrist.
medical_history.family_history: Denies psychiatric illness but mentions an aunt who committed suicide.
medical_history.social_history: Denies tobacco use. Reports occasional alcohol consumption (4 beers last night; first drink at age 14) with a history of blackouts. Previous cocaine use, last snorted on ___, and stated 9 months of sobriety prior to relapse. Born into a military family, supportive family structure including multiple aunts and a godmother. Never married but has a son living with mother. Currently engaged and living in a rooming house.

physical_examination.general_examination: Neurological findings show normal gait and tone; no rigidity observed. Mental status indicates the patient is alert but appears anxious and paranoid. Mood is reported as 'very depressed.'
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Mental status shows circumstantial and tangential thought process; endorsements of auditory and visual hallucinations. Denies suicidal and homicidal ideation but has racing thoughts and negative intrusive thoughts.
